pages.title: Propeller vs Rithmm: AI Player Prop Tools Compared (2026)
pages.description: Compare Propeller and Rithmm for AI player-prop research, DFS platform fit, plain-language analysis, models, pricing, and line comparison.
pages.hub_summary: DFS-first player-prop research versus custom predictive models, game markets, line shopping, and tracked-play workflows.
pages.headline: Propeller vs Rithmm
pages.deck: Propeller is the simpler DFS-first research workspace. Rithmm is the broader predictive-model platform for users who want game markets, custom models, sportsbook prices, and tracked plays.
pages.verdict: Choose Propeller when you want a free, plain-language player-prop workflow designed around DFS and pick'em platforms. Choose Rithmm when custom models, game predictions, sportsbook line comparison, and play tracking matter more than a focused DFS experience.
pages.verdict_note: No universal winner. These products solve overlapping but different research jobs.
pages.competitor_best: Custom predictive models, game and player-prop analysis, line shopping, and tracked-play workflows.
pages.competitor_summary: Rithmm presents win chance, edge, model reasoning, sportsbook prices, and multiple ways to build or copy predictive models. Its official site says the experience is intended to make those concepts accessible to everyday bettors.
pages.propeller_summary: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms. Its core experience uses direct player-prop language and does not require users to understand betting concepts such as expected value, devig, sharp books, or arbitrage.
pages.jargon_callout: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow. Users can research a listed player prop and read the directional evidence without first learning sportsbook pricing terminology.

rows.label: Core job
rows.propeller: Player-prop research for DFS and pick'em decisions.
rows.competitor: Predictive models for player props, spreads, totals, and moneylines.

rows.label: Plain-language experience
rows.propeller: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow. No EV, devig, sharp-book, or arbitrage vocabulary is required.
rows.competitor: Explains model reasoning in plain language, while still using win chance, edge, price, and sportsbook concepts.

rows.label: DFS / pick'em fit
rows.propeller: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms such as PrizePicks, Underdog, and DraftKings Pick6.
rows.competitor: Supports player props and prediction markets, but its product also centers sportsbook lines and one-tap sportsbook paths.

rows.label: Analysis style
rows.propeller: Sport-specific signals, directional confidence context, methodology, and documented limits.
rows.competitor: AI predictions, win probabilities, model edge, Smart Signals, and model reasoning.

rows.label: Model control
rows.propeller: Curated Propeller methodology; users inspect the evidence rather than build model weights.
rows.competitor: Build a model, copy a leaderboard model, or ask Rithmm AI to create one.

rows.label: Line comparison
rows.propeller: Shows available market context where supported; not a sportsbook line-shopping product.
rows.competitor: Compares prices across supported sportsbooks and prediction markets and links users to where they play.

rows.label: Access and price
rows.propeller: Free access is available; current terms are shown at signup.
rows.competitor: Seven-day free trial. Official page showed Core at $19.99/month billed annually and Premium at $83.33/month billed annually when checked.

rows.label: Wager placement
rows.propeller: No wagers, entries, or sportsbook execution. Research only.
rows.competitor: Does not take wagers, but provides one-tap paths to supported sportsbooks or trading platforms.

propeller_wins: A focused DFS and pick'em player-prop workflow
Plain language without required betting jargon
Useful free research before a paid commitment
No sportsbook execution or wagering flow
competitor_wins: Model leaderboards and advanced premium controls

sources.label: Rithmm official product and pricing page

faqs.q: Is Propeller or Rithmm better for DFS platforms?
faqs.a: Propeller is the more focused choice for users researching player props for DFS and pick'em platforms because that workflow shaped the product and its language. Rithmm supports player props and prediction markets, but it also serves broader sportsbook and game-market research.

faqs.q: Does Propeller use betting jargon?
faqs.a: Propeller does not require confusing betting jargon in its core workflow. The product explains player-prop evidence and directional confidence in plain language. Technical methodology remains available for users who want it.

faqs.q: Can I build my own model in Propeller?
faqs.a: No. Propeller provides its own sport-specific research methodology and inspectable evidence. Rithmm is the stronger choice if building, copying, or customizing predictive models is a requirement.

pages.title: Propeller vs Outlier: Player Prop Research Compared (2026)
pages.description: Compare Propeller and Outlier for DFS player props, plain-language research, trends, injuries, odds movement, EV tools, pricing, and workflow fit.
pages.hub_summary: Plain-language DFS research versus a deeper market-data cockpit for trends, injuries, odds movement, EV, and alerts.
pages.headline: Propeller vs Outlier
pages.accent: Focused DFS research or a market-data cockpit?
pages.deck: Propeller keeps the player-prop decision simple and DFS-first. Outlier offers a deeper market-intelligence workspace with trends, injuries, odds movement, alerts, EV indicators, arbitrage, and sportsbook execution paths.
pages.verdict: Choose Propeller for a simpler, free player-prop research workflow designed for DFS and pick'em platforms. Choose Outlier when you want a paid, data-dense command center for odds movement, injury research, sportsbook comparison, alerts, EV, arbitrage, and middling.
pages.verdict_note: Outlier offers more market tooling. Propeller removes more complexity.
pages.competitor_best: Deep trends, injuries, odds movement, alerts, sportsbook comparison, and advanced market tools.
pages.competitor_summary: Outlier combines thousands of props and games with data visuals, injury reports, odds tracking, alerts, Positive EV features, sharp-book odds, arbitrage, and middling at higher tiers. Its official site also says it compares prices across sportsbooks and DFS apps.
pages.propeller_summary: Propeller is intentionally narrower. It was designed to help users research player lines on DFS and pick'em platforms without requiring a sportsbook vocabulary or turning the experience into a trading terminal.
pages.jargon_callout: Propeller does not make users decode terms such as DVIG, no-vig, EV thresholds, sharp-book odds, middling, or arbitrage to use the core product. The advanced concepts are educational context—not the interface's starting point.

rows.label: Core job
rows.propeller: Player-prop research for DFS and pick'em decisions.
rows.competitor: Broad prop, game, odds, trend, injury, alert, and market-value research.

rows.label: Plain-language experience
rows.propeller: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow.
rows.competitor: Strong visuals and education, with advanced tiers using EV, DVIG/no-vig, sharp-book, arbitrage, and middling terminology.

rows.label: DFS / pick'em fit
rows.propeller: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms; it has no sportsbook execution flow.
rows.competitor: Compares odds across sportsbooks and DFS apps, but also emphasizes sportsbook research and two-click execution.

rows.label: Research depth
rows.propeller: Sport-specific signals, confidence context, methodology, and evidence.
rows.competitor: Trends, splits, injuries, matchup data, public sentiment, odds movement, filters, and notifications.

rows.label: Advanced market tools
rows.propeller: Does not offer arbitrage, middling, sharp-book feeds, or stake calculations.
rows.competitor: Premium+ and Pro add EV indicators, sharp-book odds, boost tools, arbitrage, and middling.

rows.label: Line comparison
rows.propeller: Market context where supported; no broad odds-shopping promise.
rows.competitor: Odds comparisons, line movement charts, and price comparison across sportsbooks and DFS apps.

rows.label: Access and price
rows.propeller: Free access is available; current terms are shown at signup.
rows.competitor: Seven-day free trial. Official pages showed Premium $19.99/month, Premium+ $29.99/month, and Pro $79.99/month when checked.

rows.label: Wager placement
rows.propeller: Research only; does not accept wagers or send users through a sportsbook execution flow.
rows.competitor: Does not take wagers, but advertises two-click placement through supported sportsbooks.

competitor_wins: Positive EV, sharp-book, arbitrage, and middling tools

sources.label: Outlier official product and plan page

faqs.q: Is Propeller or Outlier better for PrizePicks and Underdog research?
faqs.a: Propeller is the simpler DFS-first choice for direct player-prop research. Outlier can also compare DFS-app lines and provides much deeper market, trend, injury, odds-movement, and alert tooling for users who want a broader paid workspace.

faqs.q: Why does Propeller avoid betting jargon?
faqs.a: Propeller was designed for people using DFS and pick'em platforms, where the immediate question is usually how to research a listed player line. The core workflow therefore uses plain language instead of requiring EV, devig, sharp-book, arbitrage, or middling terminology.

faqs.q: Does Propeller replace Outlier Pro?
faqs.a: No. Propeller does not offer Outlier Pro features such as arbitrage feeds, middling tools, sharp-book odds, or advanced EV filters. It is a more focused player-prop research product.

pages.title: Propeller vs PlayerProps.ai: AI Prop Tools Compared (2026)
pages.description: Compare Propeller and PlayerProps.ai for DFS player props, PrizePicks workflows, plain-language analysis, projections, pricing, free access, and evidence.
pages.hub_summary: Transparent free DFS research versus a paid prediction suite with a PrizePicks optimizer, historical data, and Discord.
pages.headline: Propeller vs PlayerProps.ai
pages.accent: Transparent DFS research or a prediction suite?
pages.deck: Both products address AI-assisted player-prop research. Propeller emphasizes a free, plain-language DFS workflow and documented limits. PlayerProps.ai combines a prop predictor, PrizePicks optimizer, game predictors, historical data, and a paid community.
pages.verdict: Choose Propeller when you want free, plain-language DFS and pick'em research with visible methodology and limitations. Choose PlayerProps.ai when a PrizePicks optimizer, game-market predictors, historical data, and a large Discord community justify a paid subscription.
pages.verdict_note: Closest category overlap of the pilot; compare workflow and evidence, not marketing superlatives.
pages.competitor_best: AI prop projections, PrizePicks optimization, game predictors, historical data, and community access.
pages.competitor_summary: PlayerProps.ai shows projected direction, probability, sportsbook line and odds, model accuracy, implied probability, projection difference, recent hit rates, and line movement. Paid plans include its PrizePicks optimizer, game-market predictors, historical data, education, and Discord.
pages.propeller_summary: Propeller was designed around researching player lines for DFS and pick'em platforms. It avoids making sportsbook terminology the price of entry and links confidence language to a public methodology and explicit limitations.
pages.jargon_callout: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core experience. A new user can research a player prop without interpreting implied odds, probability edges, model-accuracy badges, or sportsbook pricing first.

rows.label: Core job
rows.propeller: Inspect player-prop evidence for DFS and pick'em decisions.
rows.competitor: Predict player props and game markets, optimize PrizePicks entries, and provide historical data.

rows.label: Plain-language experience
rows.propeller: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow.
rows.competitor: Beginner education is available, while result cards display odds, implied probability, projection difference, and accuracy labels.

rows.label: DFS / pick'em fit
rows.propeller: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms including PrizePicks, Underdog, and DraftKings Pick6.
rows.competitor: Includes a dedicated PrizePicks optimizer and player-prop predictor alongside sportsbook and game-market tools.

rows.label: Free usefulness
rows.propeller: Free access is available and core research pages remain publicly inspectable.
rows.competitor: Limited free tool previews; full unrestricted access requires a Week Pass or subscription.

rows.label: Prediction display
rows.propeller: Directional confidence context with a stated distinction from calibrated win probability.
rows.competitor: Projected side, probability, line, odds, accuracy rating, implied probability, and projection difference.

rows.label: Optimizer
rows.propeller: No entry optimizer; Propeller should not be described as one.
rows.competitor: PrizePicks Optimizer is included with paid access.

rows.label: Access and price
rows.propeller: Free access is available; current terms are shown at signup.
rows.competitor: Limited free previews. Official pricing showed $20 Week Pass, $59/month, or $499/year when checked.

rows.label: Community
rows.propeller: Product, research pages, methodology, and public evidence; no paid Discord is promised.
rows.competitor: Paid plans include access to its premium Discord community.

propeller_wins: No optimizer claim or sportsbook-execution framing

faqs.q: Is Propeller an alternative to PlayerProps.ai?
faqs.a: Yes for AI-assisted player-prop research, but the products are not identical. Propeller emphasizes a free, plain-language DFS workflow and transparent limitations. PlayerProps.ai includes a paid PrizePicks optimizer, broader game predictors, historical data, and Discord access.

faqs.q: Does Propeller have a PrizePicks optimizer?
faqs.a: No. Propeller supports PrizePicks research, but it should not be described as an entry optimizer. PlayerProps.ai is the stronger fit when a dedicated PrizePicks optimizer is required.

faqs.q: Which tool is easier for someone who does not know betting jargon?
faqs.a: Propeller is designed to be usable without confusing betting jargon. Its core workflow focuses on the player, line, directional evidence, and limitations rather than requiring users to interpret sportsbook odds vocabulary first.

pages.title: Propeller vs Props.Cash: Prop Research Compared (2026)
pages.description: Compare Propeller and Props.Cash for DFS player-prop research, plain-language analysis, stats, line scanning, pricing, sports, and mobile access.
pages.hub_summary: AI-assisted DFS research in plain language versus a paid stats, visualization, line-scanning, and roster-intelligence workspace.
pages.headline: Propeller vs Props.Cash
pages.accent: Directional evidence or a stats-and-lines workspace?
pages.deck: Propeller is an AI-assisted player-prop research workspace built for a direct DFS and pick'em decision. Props.Cash is a subscription research platform built around player statistics, visualizations, filters, roster context, and current lines from sportsbooks and DFS pick'em services.
pages.verdict: Choose Propeller when you want a free, plain-language research path from a player line to directional evidence and confidence context. Choose Props.Cash when detailed statistical slicing, visualizations, roster intelligence, correlations, and multi-source line scanning justify a paid subscription.
pages.verdict_note: This is a workflow comparison, not an accuracy ranking. Props.Cash does not currently describe itself as an AI prediction product in the official sources reviewed.
pages.competitor_best: Detailed player-stat exploration, filters, visualizations, roster context, correlations, and current line scanning.
pages.competitor_summary: Props.Cash describes itself as a data-driven player-prop research and analytics service. Its current product surfaces emphasize statistical observations and outliers, filters, performance visualizations, injuries and lineup context, correlated props, and updated lines from sportsbooks and DFS pick'em services.
pages.propeller_summary: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms. It organizes sport-specific analysis signals into direct player-prop evidence and directional confidence without requiring users to start with sportsbook pricing concepts.
pages.jargon_callout: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow. Props.Cash makes analytics approachable through visual tools, but its broader line and value-research experience can include odds and expected-value concepts.

rows.label: Core job
rows.propeller: AI-assisted player-prop research for DFS and pick'em decisions.
rows.competitor: Player-stat research, visualizations, filters, roster context, correlations, and current line scanning.

rows.label: Product approach
rows.propeller: Sport-specific analysis signals with directional confidence, methodology, and stated limits.
rows.competitor: A data-driven research and analytics service; current official sources reviewed do not describe it as an AI prediction tool.

rows.label: Plain-language experience
rows.propeller: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow.
rows.competitor: Visual research tools make statistics easier to scan, while odds and expected-value concepts remain part of the broader workflow.

rows.label: DFS / pick'em fit
rows.propeller: Designed for PrizePicks, Underdog Fantasy, and DraftKings Pick6 research.
rows.competitor: Official app sources name PrizePicks, Underdog, and several sportsbook line sources. The reviewed sources did not specifically verify DraftKings Pick6.

rows.label: Research depth
rows.propeller: Directional evidence, confidence context, current market context where supported, methodology, and limitations.
rows.competitor: Recent and historical performance, opponent context, advanced filters, roster intelligence, correlations, visualizations, and updated lines.

rows.label: Sports
rows.propeller: Signed-in coverage: NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, soccer, and PGA; availability varies by season, slate, and data.
rows.competitor: Current web promo lists NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, NCAAM, WNBA, and CSGO; availability can vary by surface and season.

rows.label: Access and price
rows.propeller: Free access is available; current terms are shown at signup.
rows.competitor: Current website lists $19.99/month, $199.99/year, and a $99.99/year NBA Pass. App-store trials and offers can differ.

rows.label: Where it runs
rows.propeller: Web and mobile research workspace, plus a public browser-based analyzer.
rows.competitor: Official plans include the web platform, iOS and Android apps, and a Discord bot; features may differ by surface.

rows.label: Wager placement
rows.propeller: Research only; does not accept wagers or place DFS entries.
rows.competitor: Research software that says it does not accept or place wagers. Users act separately on third-party platforms.

propeller_wins: A direct AI-assisted DFS and pick'em workflow
Plain language without required betting jargon
Free access and a public browser analyzer
Explicitly verified DraftKings Pick6 research fit
competitor_wins: Roster, injury, lineup, and matchup context
Broader sportsbook and DFS line scanning
Native iOS and Android apps plus a Discord bot

sources.label: Props.Cash official product and pricing page

faqs.q: What is the difference between Props.Cash and Propeller Picks?
faqs.a: Propeller is an AI-assisted player-prop research workspace designed for a plain-language DFS and pick'em decision. Props.Cash is a subscription analytics service centered on player statistics, filters, visualizations, roster context, correlations, and current line scanning.

faqs.q: Is Props.Cash an AI prediction tool?
faqs.a: The current official Props.Cash sources reviewed describe a data-driven research and analytics service, not an AI prediction product. Propeller explicitly uses sport-specific analysis signals and publishes how its directional confidence should be interpreted.

faqs.q: Which is easier for DFS pick'em research: Props.Cash or Propeller?
faqs.a: Propeller offers the more direct, jargon-light path for researching a listed PrizePicks, Underdog, or DraftKings Pick6 player line. Props.Cash is better suited to users who want to explore deeper statistics, visualizations, filters, roster intelligence, correlations, and multiple line sources.

faqs.q: Does Props.Cash support DraftKings Pick6?
faqs.a: The official Props.Cash sources reviewed name DraftKings Sportsbook, but they did not specifically verify DraftKings Pick6. Propeller is designed for DraftKings Pick6 research. Always confirm current platform coverage inside the product before relying on a listing.

faqs.q: How much does Props.Cash cost compared with Propeller?
faqs.a: Propeller has free access, with current terms shown at signup. The Props.Cash website listed $19.99 monthly, $199.99 annually, and a $99.99 annual NBA Pass when checked on July 16, 2026. Trials and app-store offers can differ.

faqs.q: Do Props.Cash or Propeller place wagers or DFS entries?
faqs.a: No. Both are research products. Propeller does not accept wagers or place DFS entries, and Props.Cash says it does not accept or place wagers. Any action on a sportsbook or DFS platform happens separately and remains the user's decision.

pages.title: Propeller vs Props Optimizer: Prop Tools Compared (2026)
pages.description: Compare Propeller and Props Optimizer for DFS pick'em research, AI analysis, line comparison, entry tools, free access, pricing, and mobile use.
pages.hub_summary: A jargon-light AI research workspace versus a broader mobile-first prop finder with line comparison, entry tools, ads, and Premium.
pages.headline: Propeller vs Props Optimizer
pages.accent: Focused DFS evidence or a broad mobile prop toolkit?
pages.deck: Both products support AI-assisted player-prop research, but the workflows differ. Propeller is a jargon-light web and mobile research workspace designed for DFS and pick'em decisions. Props Optimizer is a broader mobile-first prop finder with projections, line comparison, specialist entry tools, tracking, ads, and optional Premium access.
pages.verdict: Choose Propeller when you want a focused, plain-language path from a DFS player line to inspectable directional evidence and confidence context. Choose Props Optimizer when broad multi-platform line comparison, mobile prop-finder controls, specialist Pick6 or pick'em tools, pick tracking, and an ad-supported tier fit your workflow better.
pages.verdict_note: Propeller is not an entry optimizer. Props Optimizer's official surfaces describe both broad research features and narrower platform-specific combination tools; exact free-tier entitlements vary by store listing.
pages.competitor_best: Mobile-first prop discovery, projections, multi-platform line comparison, specialist pick'em tools, saved picks, and tracking.
pages.competitor_summary: Props Optimizer describes a player-prop research toolkit with AI analysis, proprietary and consensus projections, historical performance, customizable filters, line comparison, real-time updates, and specialist tools. Its platform pages also describe dedicated PrizePicks, Underdog, and DraftKings Pick6 workflows.
pages.propeller_summary: Propeller was designed to be used with DFS and pick'em platforms. It focuses on sport-specific evidence and directional confidence for an available player line instead of turning the experience into a broad odds-shopping or entry-building suite.
pages.jargon_callout: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow. Props Optimizer offers a wider toolset, but features such as +EV filters, consensus projections, odds shopping, correlation builders, and payout analysis introduce more specialized concepts.

rows.label: Core job
rows.propeller: AI-assisted player-prop research for DFS and pick'em decisions.
rows.competitor: Mobile-first prop finder, projections, line comparison, specialist entry tools, saved picks, and tracking.

rows.label: Plain-language experience
rows.propeller: Propeller does not use confusing betting jargon in its core workflow.
rows.competitor: Offers approachable search and filters alongside +EV, consensus projection, odds-shopping, correlation, and payout-analysis concepts.

rows.label: DFS / pick'em fit
rows.propeller: Designed for PrizePicks, Underdog Fantasy, and DraftKings Pick6 research.
rows.competitor: Official pages describe dedicated PrizePicks, Underdog, and DraftKings Pick6 tools alongside sportsbook and prediction-market line sources.

rows.label: Analysis style
rows.propeller: Sport-specific signals, directional confidence context, methodology, and documented limits.
rows.competitor: AI recommendations, proprietary and consensus projections, history, matchups, trends, injuries, line movement, sentiment, and filters.

rows.label: Entry tools
rows.propeller: No entry optimizer or automatic lineup builder; research only.
rows.competitor: Platform pages describe Pick6 flex-combination and correlation tools. Broad official listings support a prop-finder workflow, not universal automatic DFS lineup generation.

rows.label: Line comparison
rows.propeller: Available market context where supported; not a broad odds-shopping product.
rows.competitor: Compares player-prop lines across named sportsbooks, pick'em platforms, and some prediction markets.

rows.label: Access and price
rows.propeller: Free access is available; current terms are shown at signup.
rows.competitor: Ad-supported free tier. Official store copy indicates some access may be limited; Premium is advertised at $9.99/month. Verify current app-store entitlements.

rows.label: Where it runs
rows.propeller: Web and mobile research workspace, plus a public browser-based analyzer.
rows.competitor: Verified native iOS and Android apps. Official pages publish web content, but a full interactive browser app was not verified.

rows.label: Wager placement
rows.propeller: Research only; does not accept wagers or place DFS entries.
rows.competitor: Says it does not accept, place, or facilitate wagers. A handoff to third-party apps is not wager placement by Props Optimizer.

propeller_wins: A focused DFS and pick'em research decision
Plain language without required betting jargon
competitor_wins: Native iOS and Android prop-finder experience
Specialist PrizePicks, Underdog, and Pick6 tools
Saved picks, tracking, and real-time updates
Ad-supported free tier with optional Premium

sources.label: Props Optimizer official product and pricing page
Props Optimizer official Pick6 tool page

faqs.q: What is the difference between Props Optimizer and Propeller Picks?
faqs.a: Propeller is a jargon-light AI-assisted research workspace focused on evaluating a DFS or pick'em player line. Props Optimizer is a broader mobile-first prop finder with projections, multi-platform line comparison, specialist pick'em tools, saved picks, tracking, ads, and optional Premium access.

faqs.q: Is Props Optimizer free, and what does Premium unlock?
faqs.a: Props Optimizer offers an ad-supported free tier and advertises Premium at $9.99 per month. Official store descriptions differ on the exact free limits and say Premium expands access to projections, sports, filters, advanced tools, or ad-free use. Verify the current app-store offer before subscribing.

faqs.q: Which is easier for DFS pick'em research: Props Optimizer or Propeller?
faqs.a: Propeller is the simpler starting point for users who want direct player-prop evidence without confusing betting jargon. Props Optimizer fits users who want a wider mobile toolkit with projections, line comparison, specialist platform tools, saved picks, and tracking.

faqs.q: Does Props Optimizer automatically build entries?
faqs.a: Its official platform pages describe narrower tools such as DraftKings Pick6 flex-combination and correlation features. The broader official listings describe a prop finder and research toolkit, so it should not be characterized as a universal automatic DFS lineup generator. Propeller does not build entries.

faqs.q: Do Props Optimizer or Propeller place bets or accept wagers?
faqs.a: No. Propeller does not accept wagers or place DFS entries. Props Optimizer's terms say it does not accept, place, facilitate, or process wagers. Any handoff to a third-party app remains separate from the research service.

faqs.q: Can I use Props Optimizer on the web, iPhone, and Android?
faqs.a: Native iOS and Android apps are verified. Props Optimizer also publishes web-based research and tool pages, but a full interactive browser app matching the mobile product was not verified. Propeller provides web and mobile access plus a public browser-based analyzer.
